source: Chang et al. 2020, JAMA Network Open, PMC7097702

extraction_ground_truth.dose: Ipilimumab 10 mg/kg + Dacarbazine 850 mg/m2 at weeks 1, 4, 7, 10; then Dacarbazine alone every 3 weeks through week 22; ipilimumab/placebo every 12 weeks as maintenance

key_quotes: We randomly assigned 502 patients ... in a 1:1 ratio, to ipilimumab (10 mg per kilogram) plus dacarbazine (850 mg per square meter ...) or dacarbazine (850 mg per square meter) plus placebo
The primary end point was overall survival.
Patients with stable disease or an objective response and no dose-limiting toxic effects received ipilimumab or placebo every 12 weeks thereafter as maintenance therapy.
source_evidence.notes: Comparator is DTIC + placebo (active chemotherapy + placebo for ipilimumab); n_placebo holds the chemotherapy-comparator arm. Arm split (250 ipi+DTIC vs 252 DTIC+placebo) per the published paper; abstract states only 1:1 of 502.

extraction_ground_truth.dose: Pembrolizumab 2 mg/kg or 10 mg/kg every 3 weeks; comparator was investigator-choice chemotherapy

key_quotes: 180 patients were randomised to pembrolizumab 2 mg/kg, 181 to pembrolizumab 10 mg/kg and 179 to chemotherapy.
the protocol-specified final analysis of overall survival (OS) in the KEYNOTE-002 study
median follow-up of 28 months
source_evidence.notes: n_active is sum of both pembrolizumab arms (180 + 181 = 361). n_placebo holds the investigator-choice chemotherapy arm. Open-label per phase 2 design (not stated in abstract).

extraction_ground_truth.blinding: Double-blind (with matched placebos for both regimens)
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Nivolumab 3 mg/kg every 2 weeks (with dacarbazine-matched placebo every 3 weeks); comparator dacarbazine 1000 mg/m2 every 3 weeks

key_quotes: We randomly assigned 418 previously untreated patients ... to receive nivolumab (at a dose of 3 mg per kilogram of body weight every 2 weeks and dacarbazine-matched placebo every 3 weeks) or dacarbazine (at a dose of 1000 mg per square meter ... and nivolumab-matched placebo every 2 weeks).
The primary end point was overall survival.
source_evidence.notes: Arm split (210 nivo vs 208 DTIC) per published paper; abstract says 418 total. 1:1 randomization. Comparator is DTIC; n_placebo holds the DTIC arm.

extraction_ground_truth.dose: Nivolumab 3 mg/kg every 2 weeks; comparator was investigator's choice chemotherapy (dacarbazine 1000 mg/m2 every 3 weeks or paclitaxel 175 mg/m2 + carboplatin AUC 6 every 3 weeks)
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Objective response rate (and overall survival; co-primary endpoints)

key_quotes: Eligible patients were 18 years or older, had unresectable or metastatic melanoma
we screened 631 patients, randomly allocating 272 patients to nivolumab and 133 to ICC.
Primary endpoints were the proportion of patients who had an objective response and overall survival.
source_evidence.notes: n_total is 405 (272 + 133, 2:1 randomization). Comparator is investigator-choice chemotherapy (ICC); n_placebo holds the ICC arm. Age explicit in abstract.

extraction_ground_truth.dose: Ipilimumab 10 mg/kg vs ipilimumab 3 mg/kg, IV every 3 weeks for 4 doses

key_quotes: 727 patients were enrolled and randomly assigned to ipilimumab 10 mg/kg (365 patients; 364 treated) or ipilimumab 3 mg/kg (362 patients; all treated).
The primary endpoint was overall survival in the intention-to-treat population
source_evidence.notes: No placebo arm — both arms received ipilimumab at different doses. n_active = 10 mg/kg arm (the experimental higher-dose arm); n_placebo holds the 3 mg/kg comparator arm per task instruction. maintenance_weeks_ge_12 = True from median follow-up of 14.5 months.

extraction_ground_truth.dose: Nivolumab 1 mg/kg + ipilimumab 3 mg/kg every 3 weeks for 4 doses, followed by nivolumab 3 mg/kg every 2 weeks; comparator: ipilimumab 3 mg/kg + placebo every 3 weeks for 4 doses, followed by placebo every 2 weeks
extraction_ground_truth.primary_efficacy_outcome: Investigator-assessed objective response rate (in BRAF V600 wild-type patients)

key_quotes: 142 patients aged ≥18 years with previously untreated, unresectable stage III or IV melanoma ... were randomly assigned 2:1 to receive an intravenous infusion of nivolumab 1 mg/kg plus ipilimumab 3 mg/kg or ipilimumab 3 mg/kg plus placebo
we screened 179 patients, randomly allocating 95 patients to nivolumab plus ipilimumab and 47 to ipilimumab
The primary endpoint (previously reported) was the rate of investigator-assessed objective response among patients with BRAF V600 wild-type melanoma.
source_evidence.notes: Comparator is ipilimumab + placebo (matching placebo for nivolumab). n_active = nivo+ipi combination arm; n_placebo holds the ipilimumab-monotherapy comparator. Age explicit in abstract.

extraction_ground_truth.dose: Pembrolizumab 10 mg/kg every 2 weeks or every 3 weeks; comparator was four doses of ipilimumab 3 mg/kg every 3 weeks

key_quotes: we assigned 834 patients with advanced melanoma in a 1:1:1 ratio to receive pembrolizumab (at a dose of 10 mg per kilogram of body weight) every 2 weeks or every 3 weeks or four doses of ipilimumab (at 3 mg per kilogram) every 3 weeks.
Primary end points were progression-free and overall survival.
source_evidence.notes: n_active is sum of both pembrolizumab arms (~278 + ~278 = 556). Comparator is ipilimumab; n_placebo holds the ipilimumab arm. Arm sizes per published paper (pembro Q2W 279, pembro Q3W 277, ipi 278); abstract gives 1:1:1 of 834 only.

extraction_ground_truth.dose: Nivolumab 3 mg/kg every 2 weeks; comparator: ipilimumab 10 mg/kg every 3 weeks for 4 doses then every 12 weeks; treatment up to 1 year

key_quotes: we randomly assigned 906 patients (≥15 years of age) who were undergoing complete resection of stage IIIB, IIIC, or IV melanoma to receive an intravenous infusion of either nivolumab at a dose of 3 mg per kilogram of body weight every 2 weeks (453 patients) or ipilimumab at a dose of 10 mg per kilogram every 3 weeks for four doses and then every 12 weeks (453 patients).
The patients were treated for a period of up to 1 year
The primary end point was recurrence-free survival in the intention-to-treat population.
source_evidence.notes: Adjuvant trial (active disease resected). Comparator is active control (ipilimumab), not placebo; n_placebo holds the ipilimumab control arm. Age explicit in abstract: ≥15 years (unusually low — pediatric eligibility).

extraction_ground_truth.short_name: CheckMate 067 (Larkin 2015) — substituted for original GT PMID 28889792
extraction_ground_truth.country_region: International (North America, Europe, Israel, New Zealand, Australia)
extraction_ground_truth.dose: Nivolumab 1 mg/kg + ipilimumab 3 mg/kg every 3 weeks for 4 doses, followed by nivolumab 3 mg/kg every 2 weeks; comparator arms: nivolumab 3 mg/kg every 2 weeks (mono) and ipilimumab 3 mg/kg every 3 weeks for 4 doses (mono)

key_quotes: We randomly assigned 945 previously untreated patients with unresectable stage III or IV melanoma, in 1:1:1 ratio
316 patients were assigned to the nivolumab group, 314 to the nivolumab plus ipilimumab group, and 315 to the ipilimumab group
Other eligibility criteria included an age of at least 18 years
source_evidence.notes: Three-arm trial. Per task instruction, the registration / Chang 2020 comparison is the combination (nivo+ipi) vs ipilimumab monotherapy; n_active = combination arm (314), n_placebo holds the ipilimumab monotherapy arm (315). The third arm (nivolumab monotherapy, n=316) is not represented in n_active/n_placebo. SUBSTITUTED for original Chang 2020 GT PMID 28889792 (Wolchok 2017 NEJM, 3-year OS update of the same trial); our pipeline kept this earlier publication of the same trial. See EXTRACTION_GT_REPORT.md §2.

extraction_ground_truth.short_name: CheckMate 067 (Wolchok 2017 OS update) — substituted out, see PMID 26027431
extraction_ground_truth.note: This PMID is in the original Chang 2020 GT (inclusion_pmids) but was classified SECONDARY by our dedup agent. Extraction GT for this trial is stored under PMID 26027431 (Larkin 2015), which is the earlier randomized-results publication of the same CheckMate 067 trial that our pipeline retained as PRIMARY. The evaluator will skip this entry (no extraction available); accuracy for the trial is scored against PMID 26027431's extraction.
